test: Null Hypothesis — Same hill climbing method across 6 languages
parameters: 10K iterations x 2 restarts per language, 200 words from 3 folios
note: This is a LIGHTWEIGHT test. Full Phase 4 used 1M iterations x 8 restarts on 9 folios with compound tokens. This test uses only single-character mappings for a fair apples-to-apples comparison.
interpretation: At 10K iterations with single-char mapping only, Italian leads on bigram score but French actually finds MORE exact dictionary matches (3 vs 2). This means the Italian advantage seen in Phase 4 (9 exact matches) comes primarily from the COMPOUND TOKEN optimization (treating daiin, qokeey, chedy etc as single units), not from single-character mapping alone. The compound token step is where vino, della, miele emerge — and that step was only run for Italian. To be rigorous, compound token optimization should be run for all 6 languages. Without that, the 9x Italian advantage is not yet confirmed by this null test.
